examples: But that she is very nice and that for sure she will be voted Miss Congeniality!
The presupposed congeniality has already been lost, even in old-core urban areas.
MCCAIN: it's well-known that I have not been elected Miss Congeniality in the United States Senate nor with the administration.
Look, we'll certainly look for more congeniality and cooperation next year in making the process flow more easily.
But, increasingly, 60 votes has become the standard for accomplishing anything in the Senate, a reminder that congeniality has frayed.
